perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that, for a period of 5 years from receipt of information from the other party hereunder, such party shall use the same means it uses to protect its ow n confidential and propriet ary information to prevent the disclosure and to protect the confidentiality of both: i) written information received from the other party which is marked or identified as "confidential"; and ii) oral or visual information identified as confidential at the time of disclosure which is reduced to written form and provided to the other in such written form promptly after such oral or visual disclosure, hereinafter collectively referre d to as "the Information".
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees to protect confidential information received from the other party hereunder. Each party shall protect: i) written information received from the other party which is marked or identified as "confidential"; and ii) oral or visual information identified as confidential at the time of disclosure which is reduced to written form and provided to the other in such written form promptly after such oral or visual disclosure, hereinafter collectively referre d to as "the Information".
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'for a period of 5 years from receipt of information' and 'shall use the same means it uses to protect its own confidential' has been removed. The absence of a specified time duration introduces uncertainty regarding how long the confidentiality obligation lasts. Removing 'shall use the same means it uses to protect its own confidential' creates a contradiction because it is not specified by which standard, if not by the standard of protection of the own confidential proprietary, each party should protect the information. This contradicts section 6 which refers to proprietary information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party acknowledges and agrees that the Information is the confidential and/or propri etary and/or trade secret information of the other and the unauthorised use or disc losure of the Information could cause irreparable harm and significant injury to the other party for which that other party would have no adequate remedy at law. Therefore, each party shall have the right, in addition to any other rights it may have at law or in equity, to seek and obtain immediate injunctive relief in respect of any breach or potential breach of this Agreement by the other.
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the '5 years' time limit for protecting confidential information, creating a contradiction with the contradicted text, which implicitly assumes a defined period for information to be considered 'proprietary' and worthy of legal protection. Removing the standard of protection 'shall use the same means it uses to protect its own confidential' creates a contradiction with section 6 which refers to proprietary information. This directly contradicts the expectation of ongoing protection outlined in the contradicted section, making it unclear how long the obligation lasts, or what protections the parties must undertake.

perturbation.original_text: Upon completion of the Business Purpose and/or upon written request of the party providing the Information, the other party shall within 14 days return all copies of th e Information to the providing party or certify in writing that all copies of the Information have been destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon completion of the Business Purpose and/or upon written request of the party providing the Information, the other party shall return all copies of th e Information to the providing party or certify in writing that all copies of the Information have been destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'within 14 days' has been removed. The absence of a time frame for returning the information creates a contradiction because it makes enforcement ambiguous. Without a deadline, it's unclear when the obligation to return or destroy information is triggered and if a party is in breach, contradicting the purpose of section 6 related to injunctive relief for breach of agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party acknowledges and agrees that the Information is the confidential and/or propri etary and/or trade secret information of the other and the unauthorised use or disc losure of the Information could cause irreparable harm and significant injury to the other party for which that other party would have no adequate remedy at law. Therefore, each party shall have the right, in addition to any other rights it may have at law or in equity, to seek and obtain immediate injunctive relief in respect of any breach or potential breach of this Agreement by the other.
perturbation.justification: The removal of "within 14 days" from the Changed Text creates a contradiction with the Contradicted Text. The Contradicted Text emphasizes the potential for 'irreparable harm' and the need for 'immediate injunctive relief' in case of a breach. The omission of a specific timeframe for returning information in the Changed Text makes it ambiguous when a breach occurs, undermining the possibility of obtaining immediate injunctive relief and, therefore, contradicting the intent and enforceability of the Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: 8.2 Entire Agreement This Agreement and any attachments hereto: i) are the complete and exclusive statement between the parties with respect to the protection of the confidentiality of the Information; and ii) supersede all related discussions and other communications between the parties; and iii) may only be modified in writing by authorised representatives of the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.2 Entire Agreement This Agreement and any attachments hereto: i) are the complete and exclusive statement between the parties with respect to the protection of the confidentiality of the Information; and ii) supersede all related discussions and other communications between the parties; and iii) may be modified by the parties.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'only in writing by authorised representatives of the parties' has been removed. This creates an internal contradiction because it now suggests the agreement can be modified without formal written authorization or specific representatives, potentially undermining the clause's intention of a clear and controlled modification process. This directly contradicts the nature of an 'Entire Agreement' clause, where modifications require specific formalities to be valid and enforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.2 Entire Agreement This Agreement and any attachments hereto: i) are the complete and exclusive statement between the parties with respect to the protection of the confidentiality of the Information; and ii) supersede all related discussions and other communications between the parties; and iii) may be modified by the parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement could only be modified in writing by authorized representatives. The changed text removes 'only in writing by authorized representatives of the parties', so the agreement can be modified by the parties without needing to be in writing or by authorized representatives. Because the contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text, the change effectively makes modifications easier than originally intended by removing safeguards around how modifications can be made. This undermines the entire agreement clause, creating a strong in-text contradiction by allowing for potentially informal or unauthorized changes to the contract.

perturbation.original_text: 3. No Warranty/Liability Neither party makes any warranty, express or implied, with respect to the Information. Neither party shall be liable to the other or to any other person hereunder for amounts representing loss of profits or loss of business or indirect, consequential or punitive damages of the other or of such other person in connection with the provision or use of the Information hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. No Warranty/Liability Neither party makes any warranty, express or implied, with respect to the Information. Neither party shall be liable to the other or to any other person hereunder for amounts representing loss of profits or loss of business.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'or indirect, consequential or punitive damages of the other or of such other person in connection with the provision or use of the Information hereunder' has been removed. While a no warranty clause remains, the removal of the limitation on liability for consequential and punitive damages opens the door for claims for those damages. This creates an internal contradiction because the original clause aims to limit all liability, but the modified text implies that direct damages might be pursued, causing uncertainty and contradicting the intent of limiting liability. This can be challenged by invoking section 6 (Injunctive Rights), where the harmed party by the breach of this new liability can claim for the losses derived from it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party acknowledges and agrees that the Information is the confidential and/or propri etary and/or trade secret information of the other and the unauthorised use or disc losure of the Information could cause irreparable harm and significant injury to the other party for which that other party would have no adequate remedy at law. Therefore, each party shall have the right, in addition to any other rights it may have at law or in equity, to seek and obtain immediate injunctive relief in respect of any breach or potential breach of this Agreement by the other.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited liability for indirect, consequential, or punitive damages, complementing the clause regarding injunctive relief for breaches related to confidential information. The changed text removes this limitation, potentially exposing a party to claims for consequential or punitive damages, which directly contradicts the intention of limiting liability and making injunctive relief claims more plausible as the injured party can now claim for 'irreparable harm and significant injury' if the breach leads to monetary losses beyond direct damages. The ability to claim these additional damages creates an in-text contradiction as it undermines the original intent of limiting liability and triggers the contradicted text (Injunctive Rights).

perturbation.original_text: 4. No Further Rights Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights by licence or otherwise in the Information except as provided herein.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. No Further Rights Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights by licence or otherwise in the Information.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'except as provided herein' has been removed. Removing this qualifier creates uncertainty and a potential contradiction. The original clause clarifies that rights are only granted if explicitly stated elsewhere in the agreement. Without this phrase, it implies no rights are granted *at all*, which might contradict implicit rights or understandings necessary for the 'Business Purpose' as defined in Exhibit A. Specifically the exchange of information related to service and product prices, costs, commissions, fees and ot her monetary matters are also covered under this agreement and to be kept confidential. This includes any prices, costs, commissions, fees or other monetary information offered by one party to the other even if not accepted, as well as any prices, costs, commissions, fees or other monetary information that are accepted by both parties and draw n into a subsequent contract between the parties. Without the 'except as provided herein' there is no right to provide or share this information, in turn making the contract absurd.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EXHIBIT A 1. Business Purpose: All business related support services, as well as affili ate, reseller and introducer deals and all commercial and business opportunities. For the purpose of clarity, all information and discussions related to service and product prices, costs, commissions, fees and ot her monetary matters are also covered under this agreement and to be kept confidential. This includes any prices, costs, commissions, fees or other monetary information offered by one party to the other even if not accepted, as well as any prices, costs, commissions, fees or other monetary information that are accepted by both parties and draw n into a subsequent contract between the parties. 2. Addresses for Notices: a) Party 1: As above b) Party 2: As above (insert alternative address and contact name below if necessary) .................................................... .................................................... ..................... ...............................
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 4 allowed for rights to be granted by license or otherwise in the Information, 'except as provided herein'. The changed text removes this exception, implying no rights are granted at all. This directly contradicts Exhibit A, which states that discussions related to service and product prices, costs, commissions, fees and other monetary matters are covered under the agreement and to be kept confidential. Removing the exception in section 4 creates a contradiction because it implies the parties have no right to discuss or share any pricing information, which is explicitly mentioned and covered in Exhibit A. This creates a direct conflict making the contract absurd, leading to uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party.
